chapter_context.summary: Solomon aligns with Egypt by marriage; he prays for wisdom at Gibeon and receives wisdom, wealth, and honor; Solomon displays his wisdom in deciding the dispute between two harlots over a baby.

outline.text: Solomon's Marriage Alliance with Pharaoh's Daughter
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Solomon's Marriage Alliance with Pharaoh's Daughter (3:1-2), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Solomon's Dream at Gibeon; He Asks for and Receives Wisdom
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Solomon's Dream at Gibeon; He Asks for and Receives Wisdom (3:3-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Solomon's Wise Judgment with the Two Harlots
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Solomon's Wise Judgment with the Two Harlots (3:16-28), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 3, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
